items.prompt: A singly linked structure may contain a cycle. The routine must not modify nodes and may use only O(1) extra space. Which strategy directly fits the contract?
constraints: Each node has at most one next reference.

options.text: Sort payload values and look for duplicates.
Store every visited node reference in a hash set.
Reverse links and infer a cycle from whether the head changes.
Advance slow by one and fast by two; report a cycle only if their node references meet after movement.

feedback.reason: Floyd's convergence test detects a reachable cycle without mutation or a visited-node collection.
feedback.details: In an acyclic list, fast eventually reaches null. In a reachable cycle, both pointers enter the cycle and their relative position changes until their references coincide. A visited set is correct but uses O(n) extra space; duplicate values do not imply repeated nodes; reversing mutates the structure and does not provide the stated safe contract.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.visited_set: This can detect a cycle but violates the O(1)-extra-space requirement.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_values: Payload duplicates are unrelated to structural cycles and sorting does not expose repeated node identity.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.reverse_structure: Mutation is forbidden, and head changes are not a valid general cycle criterion.

items.prompt: After slow and fast are both inside a cycle of length L, slow advances one edge and fast two per iteration. Why must they eventually reference the same node?
constraints: Both pointers are already inside the same reachable cycle.
The speed difference is exactly one edge per iteration.

options.text: Their relative offset increases by one modulo L, so within at most L iterations it becomes zero.
Fast eventually reaches null inside the cycle and waits for slow.
A finite cycle must contain duplicate payload values, which forces a meeting.
Slow reverses direction when fast passes it.

feedback.reason: Inside the cycle, the relative phase changes by one modulo the finite cycle length and therefore must hit zero.
feedback.details: Number cycle positions modulo L. Each iteration adds two to fast's position and one to slow's, so the offset gains one modulo L. The sequence of offsets visits every residue before repeating, including zero. Zero means both references designate the same node. Payloads may all be distinct, null is unreachable inside the cycle, and neither pointer changes direction.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.values_repeat: Cycle convergence depends on positions and references, not on payload duplication.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.fast_stops: A pointer following next inside a cycle never reaches null.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.slow_moves_backward: Both pointers continue following next in the same direction.

items.prompt: Select all inputs that contain a cycle under the ordinary singly linked definition.
constraints: A cycle is defined by repeated node identity along next traversal.
Only the listed next references exist.

options.text: head = null, so traversal begins with no node.
head = S and S.next = null.
A.next = B and B.next = A.
head = S and S.next = S.

feedback.reason: A self-loop and a two-node back edge repeat a node reference; empty and null-terminated one-node inputs do not.
feedback.details: A cycle exists when repeated next traversal can return to a node already on the same path. S.next = S repeats S immediately, and A -> B -> A repeats A. An empty input has no path, while S -> null terminates. Minimal tests like these distinguish topology rules from assumptions based only on node count.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.empty: With no node and no edge, there is no reachable cycle.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.single_null: The only traversal step reaches null, so no node repeats.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.single_self: A self-reference is a valid cycle of length one.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.two_back: The path returns to A after two edges, forming a cycle of length two.

items.prompt: A fresh dummy node is created with dummy.next = head. No data node points to dummy, and no existing data-node next reference is changed. How does starting cycle detection at dummy affect the truth of 'a reachable cycle exists'?
constraints: dummy is newly allocated and has no incoming data-node edge.
All original next references remain unchanged.

options.text: It removes any cycle because dummy itself was not in the original list.
It always creates a cycle because dummy points into the list.
It preserves the answer: dummy adds an acyclic prefix before the same reachable data path.
The answer depends on whether dummy.value duplicates a data value.

feedback.reason: A one-way sentinel prefix does not alter any data-node cycle or create a path back to itself.
feedback.details: Reachability from dummy enters exactly the same path formerly reached from head after one extra edge. If that path terminates, it still terminates; if it enters a cycle, it still enters that cycle. A cycle involving dummy would require a reachable back edge to dummy, which is explicitly absent. Payload values are irrelevant. The sentinel may change pointer phase, but not the existence truth under these conditions.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_creates_cycle: An outgoing edge alone is not a cycle; a path must return to an earlier node.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_removes_cycle: Prepending a node does not break any existing next edge inside the data path.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.depends_on_dummy_value: Cycle existence is structural and does not depend on duplicate payload values.

items.prompt: For a reachable singly linked component containing at most n distinct nodes before termination or repetition, Floyd's cycle-existence phase follows next with slow and fast. What are its worst-case time and extra-space complexities?
constraints: n counts distinct reachable nodes before null or repetition.
No auxiliary visited collection or skip-link index is used.

feedback.reason: Each pointer follows ordinary next edges, and termination or convergence occurs after O(n) such progress with constant pointer state.
feedback.details: Moving fast two links per iteration changes only a constant factor. It is not binary search because no indexed half is discarded and every jump is implemented as ordinary next dereferences. The acyclic prefix plus at most one cycle's relative-phase convergence is O(n). slow and fast use O(1) extra space; a visited set alternative would use O(n).

items.prompt: An acyclic list is A(value=1) -> B(value=2) -> C(value=1) -> null. A detector reports a cycle when it sees value 1 for the second time. What is the defect?
constraints: A and C are separately allocated nodes.

options.text: It should advance the value comparison pointer three nodes at a time.
It should classify null as the cycle entry.
It confuses a repeated payload with revisiting the same node object.
It omitted a sentinel before A.

feedback.reason: A and C are distinct nodes, so duplicate value 1 does not imply repeated traversal state.
feedback.details: The path visits A, B, and C once each and then terminates. A payload may repeat in an acyclic list or be unique throughout a cycle. Correct cycle detectors track node references, either implicitly through fast/slow convergence or explicitly in an identity-based visited set. Sentinel use and step count do not repair a value-based criterion.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.fast_too_slow: No speed can make payload equality a valid structural identity test.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.needs_dummy: A sentinel does not distinguish equal-valued nodes or create a valid cycle signal.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.null_is_cycle: Null is the termination marker for an acyclic singly linked path, not a node in a cycle.

items.prompt: slow and fast both initialize to head. Order one detection-loop iteration so a nonempty acyclic list is not declared cyclic merely because of initialization.
constraints: slow and fast are initialized to the same head reference.
The input may be acyclic and nonempty.

elements.text: Confirm fast and fast.next are not null.
Advance slow by one next link.
Advance fast by two next links.
Compare slow and fast by node reference and report a cycle if equal.

feedback.reason: The meeting comparison must evaluate positions produced by a valid traversal step, not the intentionally equal initial state.
feedback.details: The guard establishes that the two-link fast update is safe. Both pointers then move according to the algorithm, and only afterward does reference equality constitute convergence evidence. Comparing before movement would label every nonempty input cyclic because slow and fast start equal. Some formulations start pointers at different offsets; those require their own explicit proof and guard.

items.prompt: Floyd's first phase finds a node M where slow and fast meet. The requested output is the first node E of the cycle, not merely a boolean. Which statement is correct?
constraints: The first Floyd phase has already produced a valid reference meeting M.
The cycle is reachable from head.

options.text: Adding a dummy makes the dummy node the cycle entry.
E is whichever cycle node has the smallest payload value.
M proves a cycle exists, but locating E requires the standard additional phase with one pointer reset to head.
M is always E, so returning M is sufficient.

feedback.reason: The first meeting can occur anywhere in the cycle; the entry follows from a second equal-speed phase using the established distance relation.
feedback.details: Convergence establishes existence because both references occupy one cycle node. Its location depends on prefix length and cycle phase, so M need not be the first cycle node reached from head. To find E, reset one pointer to head and advance both one step until they meet; the distance equation makes that meeting the entry. Payload ordering and a sentinel are unrelated to entry identity.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.m_always_entry: The first meeting position varies with prefix and cycle lengths and is not generally the entry.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.compare_values_for_entry: Cycle entry is a topological position, not a payload minimum.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.dummy_is_entry: A one-way sentinel predecessor lies before the data path and does not become a member of the existing cycle.
